blocks.text: DTC P0135: LAF sensor Heater circuit abnormal

The heater of the LAF sensor is integrally molded with the sensor element and controlled by the PGM-FI ECU. The heater is energized and controlled by the PGM-FI ECU to heat the sensor element, thereby accelerating the activation time of the LAF sensor and enabling stable detection at all times. The PGM-FI ECU monitors the LAF sensor heater output (return check). If the signal logic continues outside the specified state for a predetermined period of time, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores a DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: When one of the following conditions continues for 5 seconds or longer when the 12V battery voltage is 10.5V or higher.

items.text: When duty output is 0% and there is no Hi in the return value from the LAF sensor.
When duty output is 100% and there is no Lo in the return value from the LAF sensor.
When the duty output is other than 0% or 100% and the return value from the LAF sensor is fixed Hi or Lo.
Disconnection of code (AFHT line) between PGM-FI ECU and LAF sensor
Ground fault in code (AFHT line) between PGM-FI ECU and LAF sensor
LAF sensor Heater power system failure (relay, fuse)

blocks.text: Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.

If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
